crossing_conditions: ["v(t*, r*, z*) = 0 at the first attempted positive crossing", "partial_r v(t*, r*, z*) = 0 for an interior crossing", "partial_z v(t*, r*, z*) = 0 for an interior crossing", "Ltilde v(t*, r*, z*) <= 0 at an interior maximum"]
statement: The first-crossing geometry constrains v and derivatives of v, not the independent radial slope partial_r u1.
obstruction: Since partial_r v = partial_r partial_z u1, the condition fixes a mixed derivative at the crossing but does not determine partial_r u1 itself.
missing_input: ["identity or theorem linking partial_r u1 to the axial crossing data"]
consequence: The commutator sign is not fixed by the standard first-crossing maximum-principle ledger.

crossing_conditions: ["u1 has the same axial first-crossing jet for v", "partial_z u1 vanishes at the crossing point", "partial_r partial_z u1 is fixed at the crossing point"]
statement: Two local profiles can share the same v-crossing data while having opposite signs of partial_r u1 at the same point.
obstruction: Adding a radial-only perturbation with zero z-derivative leaves the v crossing unchanged but changes partial_r u1.
missing_input: ["radial monotonicity or radial-gradient coupling assumption", "compatibility with the axisymmetric Biot-Savart/transport system"]
consequence: The factor partial_r u1 remains free enough to flip the sign of -(partial_z u^r) partial_r u1 when partial_z u^r is nonzero.

crossing_conditions: ["Gamma = r u^theta = r^2 u1 obeys a maximum-principle-type bound", "Gamma amplitude control is available along the monitored route"]
statement: A maximum principle for Gamma supplies amplitude control but does not supply pointwise sign control of partial_r u1.
obstruction: Bounds on Gamma or u1 do not prevent steep local radial slopes, nor do they align partial_r u1 with partial_z u^r at a v crossing.
missing_input: ["gradient estimate strong enough at first v crossings", "sign-preserving conversion from Gamma control to partial_r u1 control"]
consequence: Gamma control is insufficient to make the commutator favorable or dominated in the v barrier equation.

crossing_conditions: ["first positive crossing may occur away from the axis", "annular mass and boundary layers may influence the local velocity gradient", "interior axial crossing data do not exhaust boundary-adjacent geometry"]
statement: Annular or boundary-adjacent crossings can inherit radial slopes from domain geometry that are invisible to axial crossing conditions.
obstruction: Boundary and annular contributions can change partial_r u1 or partial_z u^r without violating the axial first-crossing data for v.
missing_input: ["boundary-compatible radial-gradient estimate", "annular exclusion or domination theorem for the commutator"]
consequence: The commutator remains a hazard outside any purely axial/local crossing argument.

crossing_conditions: ["all admissible first positive v crossings on the monitored route", "including interior, annular, and boundary-compatible cases"]
statement: A separate theorem must couple first axial positive-crossing conditions to partial_r u1 strongly enough to sign or dominate -(partial_z u^r) partial_r u1.
obstruction: No such theorem is supplied by this ledger, and the previous rows show why it is not a formal consequence of axial crossing geometry or Gamma amplitude control.
missing_input: ["RadialGradientCouplingAtFirstAxialPositiveCrossing", "constants compatible with the v-barrier margin", "coverage of annular and boundary crossing regimes"]
consequence: The v-barrier route cannot be globally promoted; the commutator is recorded as sign-indefinite pending the missing theorem.
